hawkish_phrases: inflationary pressures in domestic prices and wages that could indicate greater persistence and thus justifies a further forceful monetary policy response
further increases in Bank Rate may be required for a sustainable return of inflation to target
if the outlook suggests more persistent inflationary pressures, it will respond forcefully, as necessary
private sector regular pay picked up further in the three months to October, to 6.9%, 0.5 percentage points stronger than the expectation
Annual food and non-alcoholic beverage price inflation had been 16.4% in November, its highest level in 45 years
dovish_phrases: UK economy was expected to be in recession for a prolonged period
inflation was expected to fall sharply from mid-2023, to some way below the 2% target
real economy remained weak, as a result of falling real incomes and tighter financial conditions
sizeable impacts from past rate increases were still to come through
household consumption remains weak and most housing market indicators have continued to soften
summary: The MPC voted 6-3 to raise Bank Rate by 50bp to 3.5%, with two members preferring no change and one preferring 75bp, stating that further increases may be required and emphasizing persistent domestic wage and price pressures despite expectations of prolonged recession.
